varieties.name: Red Rhapsody
varieties.origin: Queensland Department of Agriculture and Fisheries (QDAF), Australia, 2000s
varieties.traits: Very high-yielding, winter-fruiting variety bred specifically for Southeast Queensland conditions. Medium to large conical berries with glossy red exterior and deep red interior.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm texture, sweet flavor, attractive appearance. Excellent shelf life and transport qualities.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high - one of the top-yielding varieties for subtropical production. Dominant commercial variety in Queensland.
varieties.availability: Licensed nurseries throughout Queensland and Australia via Australasian Plant Genetics.

varieties.name: Stella-ASBP
varieties.traits: Large fruit size, bright red attractive appearance. Named after disability activist Stella Young. Shows resistance to red leaf disorder.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Beautiful flavor with consistent sweetness. High consumer rating in taste tests. Firm, glossy berries.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield, cost-effective for growers. Very productive for subtropical conditions.
varieties.availability: Licensed nurseries in Queensland via Australasian Plant Genetics. Recently released (2024).

varieties.name: Susie-ASBP (Suzie)
varieties.origin: Australian Strawberry Breeding Program (ASBP), named after Olympic swimmer Susie O'Neill
varieties.traits: Large fruit with excellent quality. Produces consistently throughout the subtropical growing season.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, firm berries with good flavor. Attractive bright red color inside and out.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield with large average fruit size. Commercial viability proven.
varieties.availability: Licensed nurseries via Australasian Plant Genetics throughout Australia.

varieties.name: Sweet Charlie
varieties.traits: Heat-tolerant variety developed for warm climates. Early season producer with glossy, large berries.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet flavor with firm texture. Medium to large conical berries. Bright red color.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity, especially in subtropical and warm climates where other varieties struggle.

varieties.name: Redlands Joy
varieties.traits: Exceptionally large berries with sweet, juicy characteristics. Mid-season variety. Shows some rain resistance.
varieties.flesh_fruit: One of the sweetest strawberries in Australia. Large size, juicy texture, excellent flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: Good performer in subtropical climate. Reliable production.
varieties.availability: Queensland and Australian nurseries, particularly popular in Southeast Queensland.

varieties.name: Sugarbaby
varieties.traits: Mid-season variety. Exceptionally attractive red, glossy, conical berries. Large to extra-large size (20g average).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright red flesh, low acidity, excellent sweet flavor. Firm and resilient with resistance to rain damage. Received highest score in Better Berries Field Day taste tests at Redlands in 2003.
varieties.yield_notes: Fruit well displayed and easily harvested. Good productivity for niche market sales.
varieties.availability: Propagated by Sweets Strawberry Runners. Protected by Plant Breeders Rights (PBR).

varieties.name: Rubygem
varieties.traits: Short-day variety producing from late autumn through early spring into summer. Low chill requirement.
varieties.flesh_fruit: High yields of moderately firm, attractive, juicy and delicious red fruit.
varieties.yield_notes: High yield producer suitable for Southeast Queensland strawberry-growing district.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries and specialized strawberry plant suppliers.

varieties.name: Sundrench
varieties.origin: Queensland Department of Agriculture and Fisheries (QDAF), Australia
varieties.traits: Similar characteristics to Red Rhapsody with deep red exterior. High yielding variety for winter growing.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep red color throughout, good flavor and firmness.
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding for subtropical winter conditions.
varieties.availability: Licensed nurseries throughout Queensland via commercial propagators.

varieties.name: Albion
varieties.traits: Day-neutral variety. Long, conical, symmetrical berries. Firm fruit bursting with sweetness. Most commonly planted variety in Victoria, Australia.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet with high sugar content. Large, firm berries with excellent shelf life. Consistent flavor profile.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields with good early season production. Produces continuously throughout growing season.
varieties.availability: Widely available from Australian nurseries and online strawberry plant retailers.

varieties.name: Festival
varieties.traits: Short-day variety with conic-shaped fruit. Deep red, glossy exterior.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Bright red interior, very firm texture, excellent flavor. Good for fresh market and processing.
varieties.yield_notes: Medium yield with smaller fruit size compared to some other varieties tested in subtropical Queensland.
varieties.availability: Available from select Australian nurseries specializing in strawberry varieties.

varieties.name: Fortuna
varieties.traits: Medium-sized berries with good appearance and firmness.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor, firm texture. Smaller fruit compared to some varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields suitable for commercial production in subtropical areas.

varieties.name: Alinta
varieties.traits: Australian-bred variety producing large, glossy berries. Disease-resistant. Long fruiting period (~9 months in most areas).
varieties.flesh_fruit: Fine flavor, medium to large berries. Good eating quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Extended fruiting season provides steady production over many months.
varieties.availability: Australian nurseries, particularly home garden suppliers.

varieties.name: Tioga
varieties.traits: Historic variety, over 50 years old. Large berries on vigorous plants.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet fruit with good flavor. Large size, good firmness.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding variety. Second highest yield after Chandler in many trials.

varieties.name: Mara des Bois
varieties.origin: France (Jacques Marionnet), 1991. Lineage: (Gento × Ostara) × (Red Gauntlet × Korona)
varieties.traits: Everbearing variety with intense wild strawberry aroma. Small to medium berries (11-16g). Round to slightly conical shape.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptional flavor - highest rated among everbearers. Intense aroma reminiscent of wild strawberries. Glossy red exterior, red flesh. Melting texture combining firmness of conventional with tenderness of wild strawberry.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yield but superior quality. Produces throughout the season. Lower yielding specialty variety.
varieties.availability: Available from select Australian nurseries, specialty berry suppliers, and online retailers. Popular home garden variety.

varieties.name: Cambridge Rival
varieties.origin: Cambridge, UK, mid-1900s. Cross between 'Dorset' and 'Early Cambridge' (both old UK varieties)
varieties.traits: Historic heirloom variety, once widely available in Australia. Saved from obscurity by Diggers Club.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Considered by many to be the best tasting strawberry in Australia. Superior flavor to modern varieties.
varieties.yield_notes: Good home garden production. Long fruiting period.

varieties.name: San Andreas
varieties.traits: Day-neutral variety. High quality fruit produced early in season. Slightly lighter color than Albion.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet with similar flavor profile to Albion. Found to be one of the sweetest varieties in trials.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields with good early production. Continuous fruiting through season.

varieties.name: Alexandria
varieties.origin: Europe, heirloom alpine variety referenced in 19th century literature
varieties.traits: Small, elongated red berries (~1 inch long × 0.5 inch wide). Clumping variety, produces no runners. Compact plants to 20cm tall.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Exceptional flavor with signature wild strawberry taste - bright and rich. Sweet-tart with intense aromatics.
varieties.yield_notes: Everbearing, produces continuously June-September. Fruits in first year from seed.

varieties.name: Yellow Wonder (White Soul)
varieties.traits: Oblong yellow/white berries. Small spreading plants to 15cm tall. Less attractive to birds than red varieties.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Luscious pineapple-vanilla flavor with rosy-floral aromatics. Very aromatic with hints of tropical fruits. Intensely sweet.
varieties.yield_notes: Everbearing, clumping variety. Continuous production through growing season.

varieties.name: Mignonette
varieties.traits: Compact variety producing masses of tiny sweet strawberries. Runner-less, forming neat clumps.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Small red berries with intense wild strawberry flavor. Very sweet and aromatic.
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific producer of small berries throughout summer. Heavy bearer for size.
varieties.availability: Seeds from specialty alpine strawberry suppliers, heirloom seed companies.

varieties.name: Baron Solemacher
varieties.traits: Classic German heirloom alpine variety. Small plants, runner-less.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet and aromatic fruits with traditional wild strawberry flavor.
varieties.yield_notes: Everbearing, produces throughout season from first year.

varieties.name: Sweet Charlie
varieties.traits: Heat-tolerant variety specifically developed for warm climates. Early season producer. Glossy large berries.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet flavor, firm texture. Medium to large conical berries. Bright red color.
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in warm climates where other varieties fail. Popular in tropical and subtropical regions.

varieties.name: Camarosa
varieties.traits: Short-day variety. One of the most popular heat-tolerant varieties. Large, juicy, sweet berries.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Huge berries, excellent sweetness, very juicy. Firm texture suitable for transport. Perfect appearance.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields in suitable conditions. One of the top-producing varieties globally.

varieties.name: Chandler
varieties.traits: June-bearing variety. One of the highest-yielding varieties. Large berries with excellent sweet flavor.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Semi-early, delicious sweet fruit. Medium to large size, medium firmness. Exceptional eating quality.
varieties.yield_notes: Highest yield variety in many Indian trials. Very productive in appropriate conditions.

varieties.name: Winter Dawn
varieties.traits: Short-day variety suited to winter production in warmer regions. Good adaptation to varied structures.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor and appearance. Medium to large berries.
varieties.yield_notes: 175-245 quintals/hectare productivity range recorded in Indian trials. Good commercial yields.

varieties.name: Camino Real
varieties.traits: Short-day variety producing extended fruit in arid subtropical climates when treated appropriately. Similar pattern to Camarosa but later fruiting.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Good flavor and appearance. Suitable for warm-climate production.
varieties.yield_notes: Productive in subtropical conditions with proper cultivation methods.

varieties.name: Sea Scape
varieties.traits: Everbearing variety not sensitive to day length. Does well in tropics according to Burgess Seed Company.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Large flavorful berries with attractive glossy finish. Good quality fruit.
varieties.yield_notes: Good everbearing production. Concentrated yields in late spring with high fall production.
varieties.availability: May need to be sourced from international suppliers or specialized Indian importers. Check with tropical monsoon climates agricultural universities.

varieties.name: Albion
varieties.traits: Day-neutral variety. Large, firm berries with symmetrical conical shape.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very sweet with highest sugar content. Excellent flavor. Long shelf life.
varieties.yield_notes: High yields particularly in second season. Good early production.

varieties.name: Tioga
varieties.traits: Historic variety over 50 years old. Large berries on vigorous plants.
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet fruit with good flavor. Large size, good firmness.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding - second highest after Chandler in many Indian trials.
